## Operational Genomic Unit Classification

### Downloading WoL2 Woltka Supporting Material

The following files have been curated to match the WoL2 phylogeny and need to be downloaded to be able to run *Woltka*.

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| label: Woltka download

mkdir -p $REF/WoL2-Woltka

while IFS= read -r line; do echo "$line"; done < ref/WoL2-Woltka/woltka-downloads.txt |
parallel -j 5 --eta "wget -N -P $REF/WoL2-Woltka -r -np -nH --cut-dirs=2 {}"
```

### Assinging reads to Operational Genomic Units using Woltka

::: callout-caution
### File format

*Woltka* does not directly accept `.bam` files but they can be piped into it `.sam` files are not available (`-` stands for stdout).

```{bash}
samtools view input.bam | woltka classify -i - -o output.biom
```

or for a directory of files:

```{bash}
find "$WOL_OUT" -maxdepth 1 -type f -name "*.bam" | \
  while read -r bam; do \
    sam="${bam/.bam/.sam}" 
    echo "$bam $sam"; \
  done | \
    parallel -j 7 --progress --bar --eta --colsep ' ' "samtools view --threads $THREADS {1} > {2}"
```

:::

```{bash}
#| label: fastp script contents
#| eval: false
#| code-summary: View script contents
{{< include ../../scripts/wol2sop.sh >}}
```

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| label: woltka classify

bash scripts/wol2sop.sh --db $REF/WoL2-Woltka --input $WOL_OUT --output $WOLTKA_OUT --outfmt tsv --filext .sam --fundbs go,kegg --compound --coverage --idmaps 2>&1 | tee $LOGS/wol2-woltka-classify.log
```

### Converting biom files to tsv

If you opt for biom format instead of tsv, you can convert to tsv using the code below:

```{bash}
#| eval: false
#| label: biom file conversion to tsv

#convert the biom table to a .tsv table to read more easily
find $WOLTKA_OUT -type f -maxdepth 1 -name "*.biom" | \                         
  while read -r biom; do \
    tsv="${biom/.biom/.tsv}" 
    echo "$biom $tsv"; \
  done | \
  parallel -j $THREADS --eta "biom convert --to-tsv -i {1} -o {2}"
```

### Calculating total features per file

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

Sys.getenv(c("WOLTKA_OUT"))

tmp <- read.delim(paste0(WOLTKA_OUT, "/ogu-taxonomy/ogus.tsv"), header = TRUE, row.names = 1)

tmp$Name = NULL #an artifact of Woltka it seems (empty column)

#re-export table without the name column
write.table(tmp, paste0(WOLTKA_OUT, "/ogu-taxonomy/ogu.tsv"), sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)

tmp <- t(tmp)
tmp <- as.data.frame(rowSums(tmp)) %>% rownames_to_column()

write.table(tmp, paste0(WOLTKA_OUT, "/total-ogu.tsv"), sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, col.names = c("sample", "#features"), quote = FALSE)
```
